commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `gateway.tools.byNode` with an in-memory session-to-node registry, applies that policy in gateway and embedded tool resolution, adds tests, and also changes browser profile schema validation to accept `driver: "extension"`.

PR surface: Source +182, Tests +248. Total +430 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection is enough for the review findings: the PR records node identity by session key and current tool construction receives run context separately; no live multi-node repro was run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author/maintainer fixes plus contributor real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not claim a repair lane can supply the contributor's missing live setup proof.

target.title: feat(gateway): restrict session tools by authenticated hosting node
target.body:
## What

Adds `gateway.tools.byNode` â€” a per-node tool restriction keyed off the **authenticated** node hosting a turn.

When a turn is dispatched from a node-originated `agent.request`, the gateway records the originating `nodeId` (from the node's cryptographic device pairing â€” a client cannot forge it) against the session key. Tool resolution then narrows that turn's toolset to the node's configured policy.

## Why

A node may legitimately drive agent turns (e.g. node-hosted intakes), but those turns should be confined to the capabilities that node is trusted with. Existing tool policy is keyed off profiles/agents/senders/groups â€” none of which is an *authenticated node identity*. `byNode` fills that gap with a restriction that is sound to enforce, not merely advise, because the `nodeId` originates from the node's authenticated connection.

## How it works

- `src/gateway/session-node-id-registry.ts` (new): bounded (TTL 15 min, cap 512) `sessionKey â†’ nodeId` map. `setSessionHostingNodeId` is called at node-originated `agent.request` dispatch (`server-node-events.ts`), using the same `canonicalKey` the dispatched turn runs under. A shared `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(sessionKey, cfg)` returns `{ nodeAllow, nodeDeny }`.
- Enforced in **both** tool-resolution paths via that one helper, so they behave identically:
  - `resolveGatewayScopedTools` (MCP / HTTP tool surfaces).
  - `createOpenClawCodingTools` (the **in-process** agent tool builder â€” which a node-originated turn actually runs through; the scoped resolver is not on that path).
- **Restriction-only**: it can narrow the toolset, never escalate. An explicitly-present (even empty) `allow` is **fail-closed** (empty â‡’ no tools); an absent `allow` means "no allow gate". `deny` extends the existing deny set. Spawned subagents inherit the narrowed surface.
- Config: `gateway.tools.byNode` is added to the TS type and the Zod schema (strict inner object; unknown keys rejected).

## Scope boundary

This PR is **only the gateway primitive**. It contains no consumer-specific wiring â€” in particular, nothing about routing any specific client's turns through a node. Any feature that wants per-node confinement simply originates its turn as a node `agent.request` and adds a `gateway.tools.byNode` entry.

## Testing

- `pnpm build`: clean.
- Gateway resolver `byNode` unit tests â€” allow-intersect, cannot-escalate, fail-closed empty allow, deny-extends, no-op when no hosting node, no-op when node unconfigured.
- Embedded-builder `byNode` unit tests â€” same matrix, asserting the actual resolved toolset narrows (added to the existing `createOpenClawCodingTools` suite).
- Config round-trip â€” schema accepts `byNode`, rejects an unknown inner key.
- **Not done:** a full live multi-node gateway run. The mechanism is exercised end-to-end at the unit level across both tool-resolution paths, and the dispatchâ†’resolution key threading is the same `canonicalKey` by construction; a live multi-node E2E is a follow-up.

## Risk / mitigation

- *Misconfiguration*: `byNode` can only **reduce** a node's tools, never grant â€” so a bad entry fails safe. Covered by the no-escalate and fail-closed tests.
- *Stale binding*: the registry entry is bounded (15-min TTL + 512 cap, oldest evicted) and is restriction-only, so a stale entry can only over-restrict, never over-grant.
